title: Emergence Simulator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive flocking simulation demonstrating how complex system-level patterns emerge from simple individual rules using the boids algorithm

learningObjectives: Explain how complex system-level patterns emerge from simple individual rules
Identify the three boid rules (separation, alignment, cohesion) and describe each one's effect
Connect the concept of emergence to real ecological systems
prerequisites: Basic understanding of ecosystems and organism interactions
Familiarity with the idea that organisms respond to local environmental cues

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive exploration with systematic variable testing
keyQuestions: How do thousands of birds fly in formation without a leader?
What happens when you remove one of the three rules?
Can you find other ecological examples where simple rules produce complex patterns?
Why is emergence important for understanding ecosystems?
commonMisconceptions: Students often assume flocking requires a leader or central control
Students may think emergence means randomness rather than organized complexity from simple rules
Students may not connect emergence in animal behavior to emergence in ecosystem processes like nutrient cycling
assessmentOpportunities: Students record observations in a table for different slider combinations and explain patterns
Students write a paragraph connecting emergence to a real ecological system
Class discussion identifying emergence in non-movement ecological examples
